candidates.target: MPI runtime/library selection and configuration for ping-pong exchange
candidates.rationale: Profiling indicates communication latency dominates (MPI_Waitall hotspot); using a more optimized MPI implementation/build and transport settings can reduce message latency and wait time without changing message matching semantics.
candidates.action_sketch: Benchmark the same binary against newer/optimized MPI stacks (e.g., vendor-tuned MPI vs baseline) and enable low-latency transports via runtime parameters/MCA/MPICH CVARs; keep rank/tag/comm/order identical and only change MPI dependency/runtime configuration.
preconditions: Ability to run the program with alternative MPI implementations or versions on the target system
Cluster fabric/transport (e.g., UCX/OFI) available and configurable via MPI runtime settings
Job environment allows per-run MPI runtime parameter changes
correctness_checks: Verify CHECKSUM matches baseline for same msg_size/iters across MPI variants
Confirm runs complete with exactly 2 ranks and no MPI errors/hangs
Validate message matching invariants (same tag=123, same peer ranks, same communicator) are unchanged
rollback_criteria: TIME_SEC does not improve (or regresses by >5%) compared to baseline for representative msg_size values
Any correctness check fails (checksum mismatch, hang, or MPI error)
